name: Defer Await
guidance: Move await into branches where actually used to avoid blocking unused code paths. Do: Move await operations into branches where they're needed. Avoid: Await at top of function blocking all branches. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: Move await into branches where actually used to avoid blocking unused code paths
details.Do: Move await operations into branches where they're needed
details.Don't: Await at top of function blocking all branches
details.Code Example Good: if (skip) return { skipped: true }; const data = await fetch()
details.Code Example Bad: const data = await fetch(); if (skip) return { skipped: true }

name: Promise.all Parallel
guidance: Execute independent async operations concurrently using Promise.all(). Do: Use Promise.all() for independent operations. Avoid: Sequential await for independent operations. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: Execute independent async operations concurrently using Promise.all()
details.Code Example Bad: const user = await fetchUser(); const posts = await fetchPosts()

name: Dependency Parallelization
guidance: Use better-all for operations with partial dependencies to maximize parallelism. Do: Use better-all to start each task at earliest possible moment. Avoid: Wait for unrelated data before starting dependent fetch. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: Use better-all for operations with partial dependencies to maximize parallelism
details.Do: Use better-all to start each task at earliest possible moment
details.Don't: Wait for unrelated data before starting dependent fetch
details.Code Example Good: await all({ user() {}, config() {}, profile() { return fetch((await this.$.user).id) } })
details.Code Example Bad: const [user, config] = await Promise.all([...]); const profile = await fetchProfile(user.id)

name: API Route Optimization
guidance: In API routes start independent operations immediately even if not awaited yet. Do: Start promises early and await late. Avoid: Sequential awaits in API handlers. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: In API routes start independent operations immediately even if not awaited yet
details.Do: Start promises early and await late
details.Code Example Good: const sessionP = auth(); const configP = fetchConfig(); const session = await sessionP
details.Code Example Bad: const session = await auth(); const config = await fetchConfig()

name: Suspense Boundaries
guidance: Use Suspense to show wrapper UI faster while data loads. Do: Wrap async components in Suspense boundaries. Avoid: Await data blocking entire page render. Severity: High.

details.Description: Use Suspense to show wrapper UI faster while data loads
details.Do: Wrap async components in Suspense boundaries
details.Don't: Await data blocking entire page render
details.Code Example Bad: const data = await fetchData(); return <DataDisplay data={data} />

name: Barrel Imports
guidance: Import directly from source files instead of barrel files to avoid loading unused modules. Do: Import directly from source path. Avoid: Import from barrel/index files. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: Import directly from source files instead of barrel files to avoid loading unused modules

name: Dynamic Imports
guidance: Use next/dynamic to lazy-load large components not needed on initial render. Do: Use dynamic() for heavy components. Avoid: Import heavy components at top level. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: Use next/dynamic to lazy-load large components not needed on initial render
details.Don't: Import heavy components at top level
details.Code Example Good: const Monaco = dynamic(() => import('./monaco'), { ssr: false })

name: Defer Third Party
guidance: Load analytics and logging after hydration since they don't block interaction. Do: Load non-critical scripts after hydration. Avoid: Include analytics in main bundle. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Load analytics and logging after hydration since they don't block interaction
details.Code Example Good: const Analytics = dynamic(() => import('@vercel/analytics'), { ssr: false })

name: Conditional Loading
guidance: Load large data or modules only when a feature is activated. Do: Dynamic import when feature enabled. Avoid: Import large modules unconditionally. Severity: High.

details.Description: Load large data or modules only when a feature is activated

name: Preload Intent
guidance: Preload heavy bundles on hover/focus before they're needed. Do: Preload on user intent signals. Avoid: Load only on click. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Preload heavy bundles on hover/focus before they're needed

name: React.cache Dedup
guidance: Use React.cache() for server-side request deduplication within single request. Do: Wrap data fetchers with cache(). Avoid: Fetch same data multiple times in tree. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Use React.cache() for server-side request deduplication within single request
details.Don't: Fetch same data multiple times in tree
details.Code Example Good: export const getUser = cache(async () => await db.user.find())
details.Code Example Bad: export async function getUser() { return await db.user.find() }

name: LRU Cache Cross-Request
guidance: Use LRU cache for data shared across sequential requests. Do: Use LRU for cross-request caching. Avoid: Refetch same data on every request. Severity: High.

details.Description: Use LRU cache for data shared across sequential requests
details.Don't: Refetch same data on every request
details.Code Example Good: const cache = new LRUCache({ max: 1000, ttl: 5*60*1000 })

name: Minimize Serialization
guidance: Only pass fields that client actually uses across RSC boundaries. Do: Pass only needed fields to client components. Avoid: Pass entire objects to client. Severity: High.

details.Description: Only pass fields that client actually uses across RSC boundaries
details.Do: Pass only needed fields to client components

name: Parallel Fetching
guidance: Restructure components to parallelize data fetching in RSC. Do: Use component composition for parallel fetches. Avoid: Sequential fetches in parent component. Severity: Critical.

details.Description: Restructure components to parallelize data fetching in RSC
details.Do: Use component composition for parallel fetches

name: After Non-blocking
guidance: Use Next.js after() to schedule work after response is sent. Do: Use after() for logging/analytics. Avoid: Block response for non-critical operations. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Use Next.js after() to schedule work after response is sent

name: SWR Deduplication
guidance: Use SWR for automatic request deduplication and caching. Do: Use useSWR for client data fetching. Avoid: Manual fetch in useEffect. Severity: Medium-High.

details.Description: Use SWR for automatic request deduplication and caching
details.Do: Use useSWR for client data fetching

name: Event Listener Dedup
guidance: Share global event listeners across component instances. Do: Use useSWRSubscription for shared listeners. Avoid: Register listener per component instance. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Share global event listeners across component instances

name: Defer State Reads
guidance: Don't subscribe to state only used in callbacks. Do: Read state on-demand in callbacks. Avoid: Subscribe to state used only in handlers. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Don't subscribe to state only used in callbacks
details.Don't: Subscribe to state used only in handlers
details.Code Example Good: const handleClick = () => { const params = new URLSearchParams(location.search) }
details.Code Example Bad: const params = useSearchParams(); const handleClick = () => { params.get('ref') }

name: Memoized Components
guidance: Extract expensive work into memoized components for early returns. Do: Extract to memo() components. Avoid: Compute expensive values before early return. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Extract expensive work into memoized components for early returns
details.Don't: Compute expensive values before early return
details.Code Example Good: const UserAvatar = memo(({ user }) => ...); if (loading) return <Skeleton />
details.Code Example Bad: const avatar = useMemo(() => compute(user)); if (loading) return <Skeleton />

name: Narrow Dependencies
guidance: Specify primitive dependencies instead of objects in effects. Do: Use primitive values in dependency arrays. Avoid: Use object references as dependencies. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Specify primitive dependencies instead of objects in effects
details.Do: Use primitive values in dependency arrays

name: Derived State
guidance: Subscribe to derived booleans instead of continuous values. Do: Use derived boolean state. Avoid: Subscribe to continuous values. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Subscribe to derived booleans instead of continuous values
details.Code Example Bad: const width = useWindowWidth(); const isMobile = width < 768

name: Functional setState
guidance: Use functional setState updates for stable callbacks and no stale closures. Do: Use functional form: setState(curr => ...). Avoid: Reference state directly in setState. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Use functional setState updates for stable callbacks and no stale closures

name: Lazy State Init
guidance: Pass function to useState for expensive initial values. Do: Use function form for expensive init. Avoid: Compute expensive value directly. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Pass function to useState for expensive initial values
details.Do: Use function form for expensive init

name: Transitions
guidance: Mark frequent non-urgent state updates as transitions. Do: Use startTransition for non-urgent updates. Avoid: Block UI on every state change. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Mark frequent non-urgent state updates as transitions
details.Don't: Block UI on every state change

name: SVG Animation Wrapper
guidance: Wrap SVG in div and animate wrapper for hardware acceleration. Do: Animate div wrapper around SVG. Avoid: Animate SVG element directly. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Wrap SVG in div and animate wrapper for hardware acceleration

name: Content Visibility
guidance: Apply content-visibility: auto to defer off-screen rendering. Do: Use content-visibility for long lists. Avoid: Render all list items immediately. Severity: High.

details.Description: Apply content-visibility: auto to defer off-screen rendering

name: Hoist Static JSX
guidance: Extract static JSX outside components to avoid re-creation. Do: Hoist static elements to module scope. Avoid: Create static elements inside components. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Extract static JSX outside components to avoid re-creation
details.Do: Hoist static elements to module scope
details.Code Example Good: const skeleton = <div class='animate-pulse' />; function C() { return skeleton }

name: Hydration No Flicker
guidance: Use inline script to set client-only data before hydration. Do: Inject sync script for client-only values. Avoid: Use useEffect causing flash. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Use inline script to set client-only data before hydration
details.Do: Inject sync script for client-only values

name: Conditional Render
guidance: Use ternary instead of && when condition can be 0 or NaN. Do: Use explicit ternary for conditionals. Avoid: Use && with potentially falsy numbers. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Use ternary instead of && when condition can be 0 or NaN

name: Activity Component
guidance: Use Activity component to preserve state/DOM for toggled components. Do: Use Activity for expensive toggle components. Avoid: Unmount/remount on visibility toggle. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Use Activity component to preserve state/DOM for toggled components
details.Do: Use Activity for expensive toggle components

name: Batch DOM CSS
guidance: Group CSS changes via classes or cssText to minimize reflows. Do: Use class toggle or cssText. Avoid: Change styles one property at a time. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Group CSS changes via classes or cssText to minimize reflows
details.Don't: Change styles one property at a time

name: Index Map Lookup
guidance: Build Map for repeated lookups instead of multiple .find() calls. Do: Build index Map for O(1) lookups. Avoid: Use .find() in loops. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Build Map for repeated lookups instead of multiple .find() calls
details.Do: Build index Map for O(1) lookups
details.Code Example Good: const byId = new Map(users.map(u => [u.id, u])); byId.get(id)
details.Code Example Bad: users.find(u => u.id === order.userId) // O(n) each time

name: Cache Property Access
guidance: Cache object property lookups in hot paths. Do: Cache values before loops. Avoid: Access nested properties in loops. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Cache object property lookups in hot paths

name: Cache Function Results
guidance: Use module-level Map to cache repeated function results. Do: Use Map cache for repeated calls. Avoid: Recompute same values repeatedly. Severity: Medium.

details.Description: Use module-level Map to cache repeated function results
details.Do: Use Map cache for repeated calls
details.Code Example Good: const cache = new Map(); if (cache.has(x)) return cache.get(x)

name: Cache Storage API
guidance: Cache localStorage/sessionStorage reads in memory. Do: Cache storage reads in Map. Avoid: Read storage on every call. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Cache localStorage/sessionStorage reads in memory

name: Combine Iterations
guidance: Combine multiple filter/map into single loop. Do: Single loop for multiple categorizations. Avoid: Chain multiple filter() calls. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Combine multiple filter/map into single loop
details.Code Example Good: for (u of users) { if (u.isAdmin) admins.push(u); if (u.isTester) testers.push(u) }

name: Length Check First
guidance: Check array lengths before expensive comparisons. Do: Early return if lengths differ. Avoid: Always run expensive comparison. Severity: Medium-High.

details.Description: Check array lengths before expensive comparisons
details.Code Example Good: if (a.length !== b.length) return true; // then compare
details.Code Example Bad: a.sort().join() !== b.sort().join() // even when lengths differ

name: Early Return
guidance: Return early when result is determined to skip processing. Do: Return immediately on first error. Avoid: Process all items then check errors. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Return early when result is determined to skip processing
details.Don't: Process all items then check errors
details.Code Example Good: for (u of users) { if (!u.email) return { error: 'Email required' } }
details.Code Example Bad: let hasError; for (...) { if (!email) hasError=true }; if (hasError)...

name: Hoist RegExp
guidance: Don't create RegExp inside render - hoist or memoize. Do: Hoist RegExp to module scope. Avoid: Create RegExp every render. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Don't create RegExp inside render - hoist or memoize
details.Code Example Bad: function C() { const re = new RegExp(pattern); re.test(x) }

name: Loop Min Max
guidance: Use loop for min/max instead of sort - O(n) vs O(n log n). Do: Single pass loop for min/max. Avoid: Sort array to find min/max. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Use loop for min/max instead of sort - O(n) vs O(n log n)
details.Code Example Good: let max = arr[0]; for (x of arr) if (x > max) max = x

name: Set Map Lookups
guidance: Use Set/Map for O(1) lookups instead of array.includes(). Do: Convert to Set for membership checks. Avoid: Use .includes() for repeated checks. Severity: Low-Medium.

details.Description: Use Set/Map for O(1) lookups instead of array.includes()
details.Do: Convert to Set for membership checks

name: toSorted Immutable
guidance: Use toSorted() instead of sort() to avoid mutating arrays. Do: Use toSorted() for immutability. Avoid: Mutate arrays with sort(). Severity: Medium-High.

details.Description: Use toSorted() instead of sort() to avoid mutating arrays

name: Event Handler Refs
guidance: Store callbacks in refs for stable effect subscriptions. Do: Use useEffectEvent for stable handlers. Avoid: Re-subscribe on every callback change. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Store callbacks in refs for stable effect subscriptions

name: useLatest Hook
guidance: Access latest values in callbacks without adding to dependency arrays. Do: Use useLatest for fresh values in stable callbacks. Avoid: Add callback to effect dependencies. Severity: Low.

details.Description: Access latest values in callbacks without adding to dependency arrays
details.Do: Use useLatest for fresh values in stable callbacks
details.Code Example Good: const cbRef = useLatest(cb); useEffect(() => { setTimeout(() => cbRef.current()) }, [])
